reviews.review_text:
Haven't played this since its OG release. An amazing gem though. Play this to realize why team Yenn and not team Tress is a thing. ;) Combat is definitely not what I remember it being (in a rough clunky way). So that has not aged well. But the overall story and experience is fun if you enjoyed Witcher 2 & 3 and want more of this world. Honestly you could just play on Easy to not deal with the combat if is frustrates you so you can go through the story. 

If you have patience to get through this 19 year old game though, it's a solid Witcher journey. Honestly I expect a remaster someday.
Even with clunky movements, the story, atmosphere, and narration carried the entire game. I can already tell the remake will be banger if it's done right, for me its an 8.5/10

♥♥♥♥ i love this game, it's an underrated masterpiece, i get it the combat sucks, sometimes it's too ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hard, yes i know but come on it's an 2007 game, first big project from a ''newer'' company don't expect a gameplay pattern of an the witcher 3 , after a few minutes you get used to it, the story it's amazing a lot of secret quests, phenomenal characters and of course the save transfer!

I started the series with the Witcher 3, continued with the Witcher 2, and wanted to give the Witcher 1 a chance despite it being very old, and i actually really enjoyed it. However, the combat mechanics and the incredibly sluggish running/walking animations truly made it a frustrating experience. For a moment, i even wanted to quit the game, but i was stubborn and pushed through to finish it. The story progresses nicely, as mentioned before, the graphics are dated, so anyone playing it for the visuals will be disappointed. Someone who has played The Witcher 3 will definitely be disappointed here, but it’s still worth giving a chance.

Playing in 2026...yep quite late but wanted to play 1 & 2 before IV comes out. 
Story felt good, characters were interesting, BG score too good. But, combat....well, simply bad. Played on hard and 90% of my fights were running around in circles. Not enjoyable that part. Anyone wanting to play this game for the first time to complete the saga, play on medium. Overall, good game.

the combat was fine honestly, its just about timing the clicks and choosing the right style based on the enemy type. the writing is great and still holds up, and the tension of choosing between the two factions was pretty fun.  the music also slaps and the side characters are very interesting, shani, dandelion and  zoltan are all great. i really liked the dice game too. overall 7.8/10

Despite being a very old game with some not so good mechanics (mainly the fighting for me) this game is worth it. Especially if you are a fan of the Witcher lore, I think you should play it. It takes some time to get used to it and I highly recommend using QoL mods so the experience is more likeable.

It may feel rough around the edges, but the atmosphere is dark and fits the Witcher pretty good. From the very beginning, the game pulls you into a morally grey world where choices actually matter, and there’s rarely a perfect outcome.

The combat doesn't feel modern or well thought, but once you get used to it, it even starts to make sense. I went with hard difficulty and after a couple of minutes I didn't had (almost) any problem defeating my opponents. There are a couple of fights in the game that were tough for me but after a few tries (well, in one of them, more that a few :D) I managed to come on top.

In the end I think this game deserves some more love, there is so many great moments, dialogues and funny episodes. Overall, this is what I liked the most - the story, it is complex, there are many twists and consequences and memorable characters, some of which we see in the next two games.

beside the silly combat that i really love. the game is peak. the only regret i have is i didn't play it sooner

I played the Witcher for the first time ever on stream and it was a sheer delight, great game, great story looking forward to playing 2 and 3 be patient with learning how the combat works and you will be in for a pimping good time ;)

Even tho the graphics are a little outdated when playing in 2026 the story is good. I've played The Witcher 3 almost 900h, read the books, and I'm really enjoying the fact that I'm finally learning the story I was missing. Glad it's still available and that it works on MacOs.

This was my very first  game in the world of fantasy and RPGs, and honestly... it was the perfect start for me in this genre. I really love everything about this game from the story and depth of events to the breathtaking world that makes you lose yourself in it for hours without noticing!!! 
Even though the game is old and the combat system was very weird but I didn`t let these things ruin my enjoyment ♥
A timeless classic and an amazing introduction into the witcher game trilogy. The stories and locations from this game are ingrained into my mind, and it still delivers after all these years.
Remake would be good. For a first time witcher player the controls are the weakest part of this game - everything else is extremely solid.

Never played Witcher trilogy in my life, so I decided to start from the very beginning. 
I know that Witcher 1 is not suitable for Steam Deck but with community gamepad layout it was made possible. So I downloaded and played.
It really feels like a passion project but it took away my joy of playing since it takes forever to beat bosses or to complete certain tasks. 
Of course that is based on the English comprehension and skill level in addition to the clunky fighting mechanics, but as a novice player myself, it was not enjoyable after 20 hours put in it.
this game makes oblivion look polished in comparison, I broke my hand, a wall, and got evicted from my apartment because of how mad this games jank made me. I love it.

Mouse flies everywhere when clicking during combat. Combos works sometimes. Most of the time the mouse just flies around everywhere.

I'm not sure if this is just a MacOS thing. Skip this game and play the sequels.

for playing and beating this game in 2026, it definitely is a recommended must play if you have never played it. Its a throw back to the past when it comes to older mmo rpg games in this era, and it is very nastalgic
im gonna be honest i thought it looked meh, but i was proven mega wrong. sure it's gameplay and grafics are outdated but it is still very fun.

🐺👍🏼
This game has some excellent moments, especially when we start interacting with our adventure companions Triss and Dandelion ❤️.

It's a slightly older game, not for everyone, but it's excellent.

great game got back to it in 2026 and im hooked.
played it on release never finished it... but now is the time played 13 hours now non stop

at first i didnt like the gameplay at all, but after i saw a video of the dev saying how they wanna make this game to be played with one hand while drinking beer, it enlighten me  and after trying again and changing the setting i get what its tryna do and teh gameplay feel more relaxing and i can enjoy more of the story i really like the game but its definetly not a game for a beginner to enjoy 
7,5/10

This one is old, weird, clunky, and somehow still has more soul than a lot of modern RPGs with ten times the budget. You can feel that it was made by people who really cared about the world, even if the combat sometimes feels like Geralt is fighting through a rhythm game only he understands.

The atmosphere is the main reason to play it. It has that dirty, gloomy, very Witcher kind of fantasy where nobody is fully clean, every choice has a bad smell, and “the lesser evil” usually still looks pretty terrible once the consequences show up later. The world feels harsh, political, superstitious, and alive in a way that fits the books really well.

I also love how different it feels from the later games. It’s smaller and rougher, but the writing, music, quests, alchemy, and strange little details give it a very specific charm. This is not the polished monster that The Witcher 3 became. This is more like the weird old tavern where the whole thing started — smoky, uncomfortable, full of stories, and somehow exactly where you want to be.

If you can handle some age and jank, there’s a great RPG here. Not perfect, but memorable, atmospheric, and very worth playing if you love this universe.
I love the fact that this game takes you back to the days of the old point and click adventure games. With the adition of the fighting style of games like Diablo

this game took me around 42 hours and i am new to the seires it was worth it litrally worth it yes its 2007  so dont mind the controls the story was good pacing environment and yeah i play iton high setting now going to witcher 2

Hud and menus are far better than the 3rd game and probably what the 4th will have cause modern devs dont like nice things. The game is definetly a product of its time but still worth going back and experiencing. I played it on the hardest difficulty and it was the clunkiest experience ive had in a video game, though once you get the right perks the clunkiness is not nearly as bad.

I have been putting off playing the Witcher games for too long.

#1 felt old and janky, but the story and atmosphere... amazing! 

Very excited to continue Geralt's journey.

Very good story so far with interesting characters. The combat is kinda junky and i am being generous here but its all right some times. Definitely would recommend it to any one who enjoyed witcher 3 and hasn't played the others yet

I have played Witcher 1 after playing Witcher3, so it took me at least 2 or 3 tries of playing it to get into it. Mechanics are different, especially fighting styles and such, but once i passed chapter 2, it was ever so smooth. Ambience, atmosphere, story, characters are all great. Witcher 3 fans would welcome the usual "lesser of evils" choices, questionable morality, reasons read between the lines, long term consequences, power plays and politics..etc Many, many pros on why to play this game.

On the cons side, inventory and looting can be tedious, farming materials is very limited by inventory space. Moving around without any mean of transportation can get too much sometimes, the teleports between Vizima quarters and swamp was very welcome.
Even if it does not look like it, story and main quests move forward quite fast, so my tip would be to (if you are into that) take your time for sidequests as well.
MEditation being limited to inkeepers and fires is annoying, especially since its best way to healup.

All in all, very very solid. Pros outweight the cons in my humble opinion.

Huh most értem a végére ismét ahhoz képest hogy egy majdnem 20 éves játék azért ennek is megvan a maga varázsa. Kezdjük először a negatív dolgokkal.

- Ami sok mindenkit elriaszthat az a harc rendszer. Ez a kattingatós nem teljesen átgondolt rendszer valljuk be azért már elég fapados. Ezt vagy megszokja az ember vagy kinlódás lesz és elmegy a kedve a játéktól nekem szerencsére sikerült valahogy megszokni.
- A másik dolog a sétálás. Ez furán hangzik mivel ez egy RPG és jó dolog lenne elveszni sétálni felfedezni stb de... az hogy csak a játék közepén van lehetőség "gyors utazni" aztán át kell menni a térkép aljáról a tetejére csónakkal és sétálni 3-4-5 néha több perceket is azért elég idegörlő tud lenni néha.
- Volt amikor több alkalommal ha nem mentettem kifagyott a játék kidobott a stemről és ha nem mentettem akkor előről kellett kezdeni pár szakaszt ez elég idegesítő volt úgyhogy megtanultam sűrűn menteni! :D

És szerintem ennyi ami negatív dolog így hírtelen nekem a grafika a legutolsó szempont amit nézek egy játékon úgyhogy azt tegye mindenki oda ahová szeretné, úgyhogy jöjjenek a jó dologok:D

-Felejthetetlen Witcher hangulat! A story, a szörnyek, a helyszínek a zenék, ezek mind tudják hozni az élményt! Nem beszélve arról hogy a játékhoz magyar szinkron is készült sokak szidják de én úgy vittem most végig és teljsen élvezhető volt és több helyen vicces is:D Voltak benne bakik de szerintem jó pofa.

Nem tudom manapság mi számít retrónak egy majdnem 20 éves játék vagy ettől régebbi címek viszont ez minden witcher rajongónak kötelező kis gyöngyszem! Akik csak a 3-al játszottak adjanak ennek is egy esélyt legalább a főtörténet miatt mert remekül megalapozza a második részt és jól felépíti a fordulatokban gazdag cselekményt!
A fantastic, classic game that helps introduce us to the Witcher trilogy. Although, it crashes frequently and the auto save system is incredibly janky, it was overall a good game.

[h2] QUICK REVIEW [/h2]
Excellent story telling game with beautiful old-school graphic while expanding it through new lore and lots of experiment in a rich immersive world. This established the first game for CD Projekt Red's The Witcher trilogy despite creating some plot holes and inconsistency of the characters based on their original book counterpart. Amazing lore? Check. Immersive music? Check. Memorable character? Check. Beautiful art direction? Check.

However, it suffers greatly from poorly aged combat and annoying balancing issue. You either pick easy mode or run meta build through guides. The learning curve is mediocre but has deep complexity. But that wont stop you from enjoying this masterpiece. Personally, I played through the normal mode and stucked at the first boss but through some guide (one time) i got no problem through the rest of the game.

[h2] WORTH PLAYING? [/h2]
ABSOLUTELY. Whether you're a Witcher fan or a fantasy geek or a RPG enjoyer or someone who just wants to hop on the bandwagon. This is a solid entry to the series, if this doesn't fit you then try read the original books or play TW2/TW3.
Pure nostalgia, played the game back in the day and absolutely loved it, still replaying it from time to time.

Gameplay was a little bit janky, but it is 19 years old, but genuinely really fun. Had a great time and the story was great, and making the decision felt like there was real weight to them. 10/10 would recommend for anyone into the books.
Pretty fun game, but everything here has be done elsewhere better
The best version of the " Witcher " as story , from here should evolve all next gens of the Witcher games as model and connected with the past !!!
The Witcher has bad combat and outdated gameplay, but the story is good enough to keep the game interesting Overall it’s an old RPG with flaws but still enjoyable for some players

One of those games that I am fully split on; after a while, the constant traveling back and forth and the combat system, become rather grating and difficult to wrangle once you start contending with tougher enemies. It is a clunky, janky game in which its myriad frictions move from charming and fun to wrestle a little with to the picture of tedium itself. With all of this said; 

The Witcher is such an enthralling narrative about power, prejudice, and the push and pull between the banalities of man's follies and the horrors of living in a world of creatures and spirits that often claim the lives of those men. The horrors bleed red and cosmic alike and the interplay between those things makes for genuinely scintillating drama and humor. 

All of this is driven by a genuinely fantastic protagonist; Geralt of Rivia is tremendously voiced by Doug Cockle in the English language, carrying weariness, snark, but also an unwavering belief in the capacity for people to be good and that their rights are unassailable in the larger scheme of things, no matter how massive the world is.

Said world is always so captivating to learn about, from its politics to its magicks to its many creatures and sylphs that roam through shadowy corners and beams of dust-billowed light alike. The narrative qualities, even if their telling is often limited by the technology and experience alotted to the team at the time, are so engaging that they are, in my opinion, often worth the drudgery and fatigue of moment to moment gameplay in many situations. There is a great narrative to be found in wrestling with its mechanical failings. 

Said narrative is strong enough that I actually do hope for once that the upcoming remake, should it ultimately be released, brings out the best in that narrative with a stronger mechanical suite with stronger parity to the more recent releases. I don't know if I would recommend the game wholesale but it really is a special and passionate work in a multitude of regards. If you're willing to contend with the aforementioned doldrums of play, then I would greatly recommend. 

Just maybe don't take up all the witcher contracts in this one.

For a twenty year old game it is quite good and I really like the story and quests so far. Its quite intersting to see how far the Witcher games have come. The only issue I have is that the game can be buggy with hair where it is glitching all over the screen, it usually is not a problem and doesn't happen often but it is a little bit bothering. Other than that is game is quite good and I would suggest that any witcher fans give it a play.

As someone who used to play World of Warcraft and Runescape, I lost in touch with those games as I grew older. Only Recently I have started playing story games again and this game truly gave me nostalgia. I can understand many people not liking the mechanics, but the mechanics are truly amazing.  
Yes there are lot of bugs, but I can truly appreciate a wonderful Story, as someone who enjoys playing games from early 2000's will appreciate the games story. It is amazing.

Story - 5/5
Gameplay - 3.5/5
Visuals - 3/5
Mechanics - 3/5

Overall, If you enjoy a good story, you will love this game.

I have to say this is an amazing game. Sure it’s a bit old and graphically not very modern anymore, but honestly it’s a gem. During the first few hours of playing I thought the game was kinda meh until I got used to the gameplay style and the combat system, but then I slowly got more into it and completely fell in love with it. The story is absolutely amazing, the characters are really well written, and the best thing about it for me was the Czech dubbing cause it’s perfect and hilarious.

If you like older games, then this game is definitely for you. And if you are a real Witcher fan, then this is basically a must play game.

8.9/10
Its good if you can get past the graphics and gameplay. the story is great and i highly recommend playing it if you like the witcher 3s story. there are ALOT of invisible walls, its not open world.

Combat takes some time to get used to, and overall pacing is slow, the player needs to be patient.

If you can endure all that ad midst the temptations of the fast paced world of modern gaming, this game rewards you with a great and memorable story, sometimes funny, and the choices you made, often even the unexpected ones, are no less impactful.

I shall continue my journey to the next title. Yes, the save games can be imported, all the way to the 3rd game.
Great game even in 2026. This is my first Witcher experience, but story was really good. Music is ameizing too.

I absolutely adore the first Witcher game. The combat is very awkward but nothing you can't get used to. The game is not too beautiful but neither hideous. The story is amazing probably my favourite out of Geralt's trilogy. The atmosphere and the music compliment it really well. Gameplay is subjective i find it to be "cosy" and relaxing, but others might see it boring. I would recommend this game to people who already have some experience with other games from the series.

Playing Wicther 1 was an enjoyable experience. The story was good and it had interesting aspects, some side quests were memorable and seeing were this franchise started was fun. Combat was sometimes a chore and in a few occasions miserable, it can easily get confused or clogged from having too many enimies around or even when you just try to play normally but the combat didnt ruin the whole experience. Witcher 1 is an old game with its own problems and challanges but i enjoyed the story and its characters. It's worth playing and i would recommend anyone to atleast give the game a shot.
Still holds up as good RPG, combat is a lil rough and unengaging but that really doesn't really matter because you're here to play as Geralt of rivia , And the game does a amazing job at that.

Игра бьівает крашится, ещё не понравилось то что буквально игра не говорит куда идти, надо самому искать путь, ещё бьівает то что нпс не появляются на локациях.
Когда она вьіходила она бьіла топ игрой но сейчас покупать этую игру можно только если вьі лютьій фанат ведьмака читали книги и т.д. либо же если вам очень интересно что тут, потому что я просто купил 3 часть понравилось, купил вторую и первую часть, первая не понравилась потому что она уже устарела и не ориентированая под новьіх молодьіх игроков.
Great game choices and actions do effect your story so be prepared for that especially when NPCs run off and get killed through no fault of your own.

Can't complain about the story, it's Geralt of Rivia. The game mechanics do show their age. Running from one end of the map to the other feels boring, but I used a mod to get around that. There's also too much nudity just for the sake of it, Though I do appreciate the "anime jiggle" animations.

The Witcher is a classic RPG game that tells the story of Geralt of Rivia, a monster hunter known as a Witcher. Even though the game is quite old, it still has a very strong story and an immersive dark fantasy atmosphere that makes it enjoyable to play.

The story is one of the best aspects of The Witcher. Players are faced with many choices that can affect the storyline and relationships with other characters. The world feels alive because every decision has consequences, making the game more interesting and realistic.
The gameplay may feel a bit outdated compared to modern RPGs, especially the combat system, which uses a unique timing-based mechanic. At first, the combat can feel awkward, but after getting used to it, it becomes enjoyable in its own way. Exploration, alchemy, and monster hunting are also important parts of the gameplay, giving players the feeling of truly being a professional monster hunter.

The atmosphere in the game is excellent, with dark environments, medieval-style cities, and a soundtrack that perfectly matches the fantasy setting. Even though the graphics are old by today’s standards, the game still has its own charm because of its storytelling and world-building.

Overall, The Witcher is highly recommended for players who enjoy deep story-driven RPGs and want to experience the beginning of Geralt’s journey before playing the newer Witcher games.

While I do recommend this game to anyone not expecting much, it is really only playable in today's day and age to get background on the characters in this series. The alchemy was super fun to learn and the characters said some pretty funny stuff sometimes. I love the story and exploration this game puts out, but The Witcher is extremely wonky in so many aspects including combat, saving the game, dialogue, and cut scenes. For example, you can only really strafe to the side in this game. There really is not a way to run side to side, only forward which makes combat feel weird. The combat in this game is just clicking on enemies and does have a charm to it after learning how to use it. There are way too many quests at times and also way too many where you have to change the time of day. However despite these flaws I still would recommend this game to anyone interested in playing the entire Witcher series. I know this rating does not make sense since I am recommending it, but when there are so many other games to play of higher quality, I can not give it a higher rating. Very VERY solid 3.5/10. Again only recommended if you are interested in starting or just getting background on the Witcher series  (which is exactly why i played it.)

I was so excited for completing this title! It was a mark of maturity for expanding what I thought was unplayable jank and can't appreciate the developers, and the modding community, enough for their unrelenting contributions to a wonderful, dark, complex world.

Once I finally got through the second chapter, I couldn't put the game down and found myself looking at the adventure series, too! lol
worst gameplay ive ever had the displeasure of playing, and that said the game is still a 9/10 for the world, story, characters and atmosphere alone. i hated playing the game but i loved the game
This game has an amazing vibe and the story is engaging. Once you learn the controls and find what works its quite fun as well.

Still holds up nearly 20 years later. Runs well on modern hardware at 1440p, though it does require a few settings tweaks. I finally finished the story with a long-running cloud save that spanned a few years. The first chapter is probably the most difficult. The storytelling is excellent, and the city of Vizima remains one of my favorite fantasy settings.

Is the combat like trudging through mud with concrete boots on? Yes. 
Was the beast the bane of my existence in the first act? Yes (took me 4 hours and the death of the entire village - Soz villagers but you were terrible people.)
But if you can overlook those points, this game is absolutely worth playing. The story is phenomenal and I can't get enough of it.

The Witcher exudes the melancholic beauty of an overcast northern european day like no other game

Absolute slavjank RPG classic

Positives:
- It adheres to the world of The Witcher surprisingly well. CDPR made sure to align with the Continent's lore and Geralt's history while also leaving room to add upon it. It also manages to tie up some conflicts from the books rather nicely, even those you wouldn't think would require more to them.
- A decent amount of choice and impact on how the story and world evolve, albeit not a whole lot in comparison to the latter 2 games. Choices include things that wouldn't be too out-of-character for Geralt to make (most of the time), and the game justifies it anyway with Geralt's amnesia.
- The themes are also very well-aligned with that of the novels. Geralt is an individual who does his best to maintian neutrality while also being forced to pick sides among the sapient beings of the world. He often finds that the monsters aren't the problem, but the people who contract him to kill them.
- edit: There's a surprisingly decent amount of "replayability." This particularly involves the skills and perks you can give Geralt as you level up and progress which can make for unique builds for each playthrough. Additionally, the choices you make and the factions you side with can shape how the end-game progresses.
Negatives:
- Smaller things like flat voice acting most of the time with some solid exceptions and stiff character dynamics. I think Geralt's fine for what direction they went with him in.,
- Repetitive, dull, and janky gameplay. It's very KOTOR-like in that it's half-turnbased and half-realtime combat (edit: a system I grossly do not like. Pick one or the other please). The level design drags, because it involves a lot of backtracking and little liberty to navigate the worldspaces. I'm not expecting an open-world experience, but this still reminds me of KOTOR in how unimaginative and frustrating the exploration and travel can be.,
- Very drawn-out narrative structure. There's plenty of bits and pieces to work with that can make for an awesome story. I don't even think this story is particularly bad, but the gameplay and the six chapters with repetitive gameplay and story beats among each one make it to where I lost most of my interest by the end of Chapter 6. No doubt there are a lot of moving parts in stories like this, but I don't think it was executed very well.,
Overall:
- Okay. Maybe a 5/10. I wouldn't consider it bad or slop, because you can tell there's a considerable amount of effort and passion put into this game and there are things to like about it. I can comfortably say it's a mid game while also appreciating that it's still art.,
- Would I recommend this? Maybe. If you got through KOTOR 1 and 2 and/or like them I would suggest giving this a try.,
If you do try Witcher 1 out, it's best you get at least both 1 and 2, because I think they're best played back-to-back.

I probably wouldn't recommend as a start point into the witcher games(i would choose witcher 2 or 3), but its a cool rpg. The story has some cool twists to it.

The game is rough around the edges(was a lot worse when it came out back in the day), but for me it is still somehow fun to play,
The only issue I have with the game is the mission design. Lots of back-tracking back and forth.
Really cool game with a good story and a pretty exotic but still cool combat

The game's story still holds up very well after all this time, the combat system can be rough and there is not native controller support but I managed to map some controls anyway to a controller and it makes the combat manageable. Overall the artwork, story, and characters make it a great game despite it's age and I recommend any Witcher fans to try it out!

At first I thought that this will be just another RPG game. 

I was wrong, I now realized why this game is considered classic. Engaging storylines, interesting characters, fun gameplay, and a lot of content that keeps me immersed for hours and hours through.

If I have to say one thing I have a minor complaint is the combat. A bit clunky at times, but It is a product of its time. So, in the end it's still enjoyable for me. Really recommend it even though it's a dated game.

This game is definitely showing its age. The graphics are a bit rough by todays standards. The combat is incredibly dated, but once you've got it the game becomes much easier. I don't think I would recommend this game to most people or really as a first Witcher game. But if you're an old school RPG fan, or a Witcher fan looking for more, Then absolutely give it a shot. I followed this modding guide. [url=https://www.reddit.com/r/witcher/comments/kg3u76/the_witcher_enhanced_edition_revised_modding_guide/]Modding Guide[/url] To update visuals and combat a bit.

The Witcher 1 feels like someone adapted a fantasy novel using a microwave and pure Eastern European spite.

The combat is just rhythm-game arthritis, Geralt runs like his knees owe money, and every NPC looks emotionally exhausted.

So, I know the problem here is that I waited over a decade after the game launched to try to play it, and that is on me. However, the controls of this game aged like chocolate milk left on the dashboard of your car in the Arizona summer. The controls are unintuitive, confusing, and just generally frustrating to try and play. The graphics are horribly dated to the point that mods don't even help. So am I saying that the series is bad? No. Am I saying you shouldn't play the newer games? Also no. Am I saying that you should try to play this now in 2026? A resounding no. If you missed out on playing this 18 years ago like I did, then just read the Wiki on it or watch Youtube videos and save yourself the hassle and $9.99. 3/10

This game has not aged well at all. I can only really recommend this game if you are a Witcher superfan, but otherwise I kind of had a rough experience playing this. It's CDPR's first game, so obviously there are gonna be some growing pains. I can see the vision, but the execution was not what I was hoping for. Possibly the worst part about the game to me though is its length, which was FAR longer than it needed to be. Admittedly, I did do as many extra quests as I could, but even without those the game is quite long, and most of that time will literally be you running back and forth across the same maps over and over and over again to go talk to someone to go talk to another person and repeat with some monster-slaying sometimes. The combat system for slaying said monsters is also drastically outdated, but it did kinda grow on me eventually. It's objectively bad though. Do note that this game has an incredible atmosphere that definitely feels the most like the books out of the three games. Overall, though, this is definitely no Witcher 2 or 3.

[b] 4/10 [/b]

This game is phenomenal. I have played through "Witcher 2" multiple times; and "Witcher: Wild Hunt" more times than I can recall; but I have never finished "Witcher 1". The reason I never finished it was because when I bought it back in 2008 my machine could barely keep up with it; having to stop in the early parts of act III because my game basically became a slideshow at 1-3 fps.  By the time I had a machine that could run it, I had already moved on to the more modern-feeling sequels...and just never really looked back to finish the 1st.

Flash forward 18 years and I can now finally revisit it on my current machine that cranks out solid 70+ fps at 7680 x 1440p ultrawide while modded to the gills.  Some reviewers have said this hasn't aged well, but I have to disagree when these mods are taken into account.  This is such a unique RPG: its combat, its navigation; it is really something I haven't seen copied by others.  Some may say that the reason it hasn't been copied was because these unique approaches were bad, but again: gotta disagree.  I have been binging this game for the past couple of days and enjoying every minute.  

This game is definitely worth revisiting.  Get the mods though...makes all the difference in the world.  
I just used one of Nexus' recommended mod lists via Vortex and it shaves about a dozen years from its nearly 20 year-old age in terms of presentation, texture quality, etc.  

The acting is definitely more stilted than in Wild Hunt...you can tell there was far more reading going on than delivering dramatic performances like in Wild Hunt; but that is really part of the charm of this game.  It is cool to hear the same voice actors in all my favorite characters early on in the lifespan of this universe

Give it a try in 2026 if you are a huge Witcher fan but could never get into just how different this first entry is.
Terrible gameplay with a bland story and characters.
Tried a couple times to give this a chance since Witcher 3 is such a master piece.  Even the Enhanced addition is absolutely impossible to enjoy.  I'll wait for a remake someday.

Very good game, very good story. The controls are a bit janky but you can get used to it. The story is well written, the voice actors are so-so, but even the worst ones never took me out of the game. There is an unfortunate misogyny throughout the game that was very typical of the 2000's but not as egregious as most other games of that era. I think male characters should have romance cards too, and they should be the same style as the ones already in the game. I'd love to see Velerad arching his back like a pinup girl. :D

totally worth it for anyone that has already played games from that time and is ok going back

the rest: skip it, watch some gameplay or something

If you're a fan of TW3 then you need to play this! You're missing out! The combat was clunky but the experience was fun! The storyline was also amazing! Jumping from the mechanics of TW3 to this was a bit overwhelming, but once I understood how to play, I couldn't stop!

Es complicado recomendar The witcher porque creo que este juego envejecio muy mal, si tuviera que decidir entre recomendarlo o no, diria que si porque al final del dia es de la saga principal y no deja de tener esa esencia que hace unicos a los juegos de la franquicia The witcher, pero lamentablemente tiene un sistema de misiones muy aburrido donde para avanzar en casi lo que sea ocupas ir de un lado a otro, variar entre horarios, personajes, casi siempre necesitas algun objeto para avanzar en la Quest y lo peor es que casi todas las misiones son asi, es un ocupo un desto para un aquello hasta que logres terminar.
